Rank/dimensionality reduction *is* learning.

Let's say you have a set of M objects, among which you notice N objects share some attribute in common. When you reify the judgement of an object having this attribute into a "kind", you reduce an M (- 1) dimensional space into a 1D (half) space.

Relevant highlight from the Jacobian Conjecture's WP article:

> The Jacobian conjecture is notorious for the large number of published and unpublished proofs that turned out to contain subtle errors.

Yes, we can all plug and chug by hand. But I can't speak to that editor's "proper" contention.
